title: SpaceX wins $2.29 billion Space Force contract for military data network
summary: SpaceX has secured a $2.29 billion contract from the U.S. Space Force to develop the Space Data Network backbone. This deal marks a significant expansion of SpaceX's role in military communications infrastructure.

title: NASA selects four companies for initial moon base awards
summary: NASA selected four companies to develop and deliver landers and drones for its lunar base plans. The contracts mark the initial awards for the agency's moon base initiative.

title: Open Cosmos’ CEO on what it takes to bring IoT to space
summary: Open Cosmos, a UK-based company, aims to deploy the ConnectedCosmos broadband constellation by 2028 to reduce Europe's reliance on undersea cables. The CEO discusses the challenges and opportunities of bringing IoT to space.

title: Starcloud orders Starlink lasers for orbital data center network
summary: Starcloud has ordered optical terminals from SpaceX to integrate Starlink's global data-relay network for its orbital data centers. This collaboration strengthens their partnership, as SpaceX is expected to launch Starcloud's full-size spacecraft.

title: The surge in military budgets can help Europe’s entrepreneurial space sector — if spending speeds up
summary: Europe's entrepreneurial space sector could benefit from increased military budgets, but bureaucratic delays may hinder progress. Industry leaders discussed this at the SmallSat Europe conference.

title: Voyager wins DARPA contract for solid rocket propellant technology
summary: Voyager Space has secured a $16.5 million DARPA contract to develop thrust-control technology for solid rocket propellant systems, aiming to enhance adaptability for various missions and weapons programs.

title: Regulations and access to capital continue to hinder Europe’s smallsat industry
summary: Burdenous regulations and limited access to capital are slowing Europe's smallsat industry despite new legislation and increased defense spending.

title: Chinese startup Mega Engine advances reusable staged-combustion rocket engine
summary: Chinese startup Mega Engine successfully conducted a long-duration hot fire test of a closed-cycle kerosene-liquid oxygen engine, advancing reusable staged-combustion rocket technology.

title: Orbit Fab, Thales Alenia to study refueling for electric-propulsion satellites
summary: Orbit Fab and Thales Alenia Space will collaborate to study the integration of Orbit Fab's refueling interface with Thales Alenia's electric propulsion systems for satellites. This partnership aims to advance satellite refueling capabilities in the space industry.

title: Exolaunch and SEOPS purchase Falcon 9 launches for dedicated rideshare missions
summary: Exolaunch and SEOPS have each purchased dedicated Falcon 9 launches to meet increasing demand for rideshare missions. This move highlights the growing reliance on dedicated rideshares for payload deployment.

title: Artemis moon base will cover 'hundreds of square miles' with hopping drones and new lunar rovers, NASA says
summary: NASA plans to build an Artemis moon base covering hundreds of square miles, using hopping drones and new lunar rovers. The agency awarded $1 billion in contracts to initiate the project.

title: 'Masters of the Universe' gets a final trailer, and it's a perfect blend of nostalgia, sci-fi battles, and muscles (video)
summary: A final trailer for 'Masters of the Universe' has been released, blending nostalgia, sci-fi battles, and muscular themes. The trailer features Skeletor's villainous declaration of taking a family's world.

title: Satellites imaged an underwater volcano erupting — but scientists have no idea what's actually happening on the seafloor
summary: Satellites captured footage of an underwater volcano erupting, but scientists lack data about the seafloor event. The eruption occurred in a poorly explored region of the ocean.

title: 'Redshift' blends James Webb Space Telescope, Artemis 2 and Pink Floyd into a cosmic journey through light and sound (video, photos)
summary: An artistic project blends imagery from the James Webb Space Telescope, NASA's Artemis 2 mission, and Pink Floyd into a multimedia experience. The collaboration between artist Ashley Zelinskie and DJ illich Mujica took place in New York City.

title: Seeing double with the Artemis 2 rocket boosters | Space photo of the day for May 26, 2026
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission showcases a space photo of the two rocket boosters mid-air after separation. This milestone highlights progress in the Artemis program's preparations for crewed lunar missions.

title: Could a cosmic uncertainty principle help explain dark matter?
summary: A new theory suggests the universe might have its own version of Heisenberg's uncertainty principle, potentially explaining dark energy without requiring new physics. This cosmic uncertainty principle could help account for dark matter.

title: Unusual red northern lights over Japan suggest some solar storms are stronger than we thought
summary: Researchers found that crimson auroras over Japan extended hundreds of miles higher than expected, challenging assumptions about the strength of geomagnetic storms. This suggests some solar storms may be stronger than previously thought.

title: HBO's 'Lanterns' does have some green in it after all, as latest trailer shows Hal Jordan unleashing the power of the ring (video)
summary: HBO's 'Lanterns' trailer reveals Hal Jordan wielding the power of the Green Lantern's ring, showcasing its immense power. The clip highlights the iconic superhero's abilities in the upcoming series.

title: 'Very interesting wiggles' in data from silent NASA Mars spacecraft lead to unexpected solar wind discovery
summary: Data from NASA's MAVEN spacecraft revealed unexpected solar wind effects on Mars' atmosphere, uncovering a new atmospheric phenomenon. This discovery highlights how solar storms influence planets lacking strong magnetic fields.

title: DARPA readies robotic deep-space repair satellite for 2026 launch
summary: DARPA is preparing to launch a robotic satellite in 2026 to repair geosynchronous satellites, extending their operational lifespan. The mission aims to test this capability as early as this summer.

title: NASA to Announce Artemis III Crew, Provide Mission Progress Update
summary: NASA will announce the Artemis III crew and provide a mission progress update during a live event on June 9. The event will be streamed on NASA+ and YouTube.

title: NASA Astronauts to Answer Questions from Students in New York
summary: NASA astronaut Jessica Meir will answer STEM questions from students in New York during a live Earth-to-space call from the International Space Station. The event will be streamed live on NASA's YouTube channel at 11:05 p.m. EDT on May 28.

title: NASA Provides Update on Moon Base Rovers, Landers, Missions
summary: NASA announced new contracts for lunar rovers and cargo landers during a Moon Base event. The agency also shared launch timelines and milestones for upcoming Moon missions.

title: Chennai City Lights
summary: A photograph taken from the International Space Station captures Chennai's LED-lit streets at night. The image highlights Earth observations from the space station.

title: New Instrument Used Antarctic Ice Sheet to Probe Extreme Universe
summary: NASA's Payload for Ultrahigh Energy Observations (PUEO) mission, part of the Astrophysics Pioneers Program, uses the Antarctic ice sheet to detect the most energetic particles in the universe. This innovative approach leverages the ice as a detection medium for extreme cosmic events.

title: A Full Moon Checkup
summary: Landsat 9 images the lunar surface monthly during the full Moon to maintain data accuracy and consistency. This practice helps calibrate the spacecraft's Earth observation instruments.

title: Jaclyn Kagey Shapes Humanity’s Return to the Moon
summary: Jaclyn Kagey leads NASA's preparations for astronauts' lunar surface exploration as the Artemis extravehicular activity lead. Her role is crucial for the Artemis missions, which aim to explore the Moon's South Pole and advance future deep space exploration.

title: Call for Creatives: NASA Seeks Help Illuminating Mission Storytelling
summary: NASA is seeking creative partners to help tell the stories of its missions, including Artemis Moon missions and nuclear propulsion, through various media formats. The agency issued an Announcement for Proposals on May 21 to collaborate with filmmakers, songwriters, and other storytellers.

title: NASA Sets Coverage for Roscosmos Spacewalk Outside Space Station
summary: NASA will broadcast a live spacewalk by two Roscosmos cosmonauts outside the International Space Station on May 27, starting at 10:15 a.m. EDT and lasting five hours. Coverage begins at 9:45 a.m. on NASA+, Amazon Prime, and NASA’s YouTube channel.

title: Following Starship V3 debut, SpaceX prepares for follow up
summary: SpaceX successfully flew the Starship Version 3 for the first time this week. The company is now preparing for a follow-up mission.

title: Launch Preview: Starlink and Amazon Leo missions fill manifest
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled this week, with four of them being Falcon 9 Starlink missions. Amazon's Leo missions are also part of the manifest.

title: Vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance keep Expedition 74 busy through April
summary: Expedition 74 crew aboard the International Space Station has been occupied with vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance tasks throughout April. The activities highlight ongoing operations and research in low Earth orbit.

title: Shenzhou 23 launches new crew to Tiangong space station
summary: Shenzhou 23 successfully launched a new crew to the Chinese Tiangong space station, which has been permanently occupied since June 5, 2022. This marks another milestone in China's ongoing human spaceflight program.

title: Starship Flight 12: Block 3 Impresses on First Flight
summary: Starship Flight 12, featuring Block 3, successfully launched on May 22, 2026, at 5:30:21 pm CDT from SpaceX's facilities. The flight marked a significant milestone for SpaceX's next-generation rocket.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Electron, and Vega C launch missions alongside Starship Flight 12
summary: Six space launches are scheduled globally in the next week, including SpaceX's 12th Starship flight, a Falcon 9 mission, an Electron launch, and a Vega C mission.

title: Vega C launches SMILE to study Earth’s magnetosphere
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) launched the SMILE mission using the Vega C rocket to study Earth's magnetosphere. This mission aims to investigate the interaction between the solar wind and Earth's magnetic field.

title: Super Heavy Block 3 the Booster of the Future
summary: The article discusses the evolution of the Super Heavy booster, the first stage of SpaceX's Starship, highlighting its ongoing upgrades. It emphasizes the significance of the Super Heavy Block 3 as a future iteration of the booster.

title: Artemis III deep into processing and planning for key HLS test
summary: NASA is actively planning Artemis III, a significant crewed mission to the Moon. The mission includes key testing for the Human Landing System (HLS).

title: Flight 12 readies for the debut SpaceX’s next Ship evolution
summary: SpaceX is preparing for Flight 12, marking the debut of the next evolution of Starship's second stage. The vehicle has undergone multiple iterations since the early test flights of SN8.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on space industry developments without specifying details. The article's content is not provided for analysis.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section tests materials for spacecraft to ensure they survive space conditions. The section is supported by young researchers contributing innovative ideas.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This technology is opening new pathways for space activities.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space mission is undergoing a critical parachute deployment test in extreme conditions to ensure safe landing. The bake-out process is designed to remove moisture and contaminants from the parachute fabric.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will slow the rover during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology is being developed for space applications, offering a novel method for capturing and securing objects in orbit. This innovation could enhance satellite servicing and debris removal missions.

title: NASA Awards LTV, Lunar Transport Contracts
summary: NASA has awarded contracts for Lunar Terrain Vehicles (LTV) and lunar transport services, marking a significant step toward returning humans to the Moon. The announcement was made by NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman during a press conference.

title: Op-ed: Commercial Demand in LEO is Lacking. The Government Can Help.
summary: A recent op-ed argues that the most promising market opportunity in Low Earth Orbit (LEO)—in-space manufacturing—currently lacks sufficient commercial demand to sustain itself, and suggests government support is necessary to drive growth.

title: Optical Links: When to Incorporate Them? When Not To?
summary: The article discusses the cost-effectiveness of optical links for satellite applications, noting that they often cost hundreds of thousands of dollars and may not be worth the investment for many use cases.

title: Satellogic Lands $18M+ Defense-Monitoring Contract
summary: Satellogic secured a defense-monitoring contract worth over $18 million, marking its first major agreement since shifting to persistent-monitoring capabilities.

title: SpaceX Launches Starship V3 for the First Time
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its Starship V3 rocket for the first time, marking its twelfth test flight with a full suite of upgrades. This milestone represents a significant advancement in SpaceX's reusable launch vehicle technology.

title: SEOPS Will Start Waymaker LEO Rideshares In 2028
summary: SEOPS is set to launch Waymaker LEO rideshares in 2028, targeting time-sensitive or non-standard payloads that current rideshare options cannot support.

title: The US Needs a New Launch Site, Air Force Secretary Says
summary: The US Air Force Secretary has stated that existing military launch sites are nearing maximum capacity, necessitating the development of a new launch site. This highlights the growing demand for space launch capabilities.

title: ESA Chief Calls on Europe to Double Funds for Space
summary: ESA's chief urges Europe to double its funding for space initiatives, citing significant progress but insufficient investment according to some industry experts.

title: Better Moon-Tech Tops NASA Stakeholder Wishlist
summary: NASA's stakeholder survey revealed that improved Moon technology is the top priority for space tech investments. The survey was conducted two years ago to guide NASA's future technology development.

title: ESA Approves Two Scout Science Missions
summary: ESA has approved two Scout science missions aimed at conducting groundbreaking research with cost-effective solutions. The missions demonstrate Europe's commitment to affordable yet innovative space science.

title: Rideshare Providers SEOPS and Exolaunch Buy Dedicated Falcon 9 Missions
summary: SEOPS and Exolaunch have purchased dedicated SpaceX Falcon 9 missions for their rideshare operations. The deals were announced during Smallsat Europe.

title: Connected Car Market Picks Up Steam With Standards and NTN, Experts Say
summary: The connected car market is gaining momentum with the establishment of standards and advancements in non-terrestrial networks (NTN). Industry experts discussed this progress during SmallSat Europe.

title: American Airlines to Equip More Than 500 Planes with Starlink Wi-Fi
summary: American Airlines has partnered with SpaceX to equip over 500 narrowbody Airbus aircraft with Starlink's in-flight Wi-Fi service. This deal expands Starlink's reach in the aviation industry.

title: Space Force Awards Viasat and Intelsat General $438M for Continued Work on PTS-G
summary: The U.S. Space Force awarded Viasat and Intelsat General a $438 million contract to continue work on the Protected Tactical Satellite-Global (PTS-G) program. The satellites will support secure communications for military operations.

title: ST Engineering iDirect’s New CEO Kuppanna Talks Ground Tech’s Cloud-Native, Interoperable Future
summary: ST Engineering iDirect appointed Sridhar Kuppanna as its new CEO in late March to lead the company's ground technology advancements amid a dynamic market. The move highlights the company's focus on cloud-native and interoperable solutions in the satellite communications sector.

title: UK Inaugurates New Space Domain Awareness Software Borealis
summary: The UK has launched a new space domain awareness software called Borealis, which is now operational six months ahead of schedule. The software enhances the tracking of satellites and space debris for the government.

title: Rocket Lab to Build First GEO Satellites for Space Force Space Domain Awareness Mission
summary: Rocket Lab has secured a $90 million contract to build its first Geostationary Orbit (GEO) satellites for a U.S. Space Force space domain awareness mission. This marks a significant expansion of Rocket Lab's satellite manufacturing capabilities.

title: Viasat Completes D2D Demo in Uzbekistan
summary: Viasat has successfully completed a direct-to-device (D2D) demonstration in Uzbekistan, showcasing messaging capabilities on Android smartphones via Viasat's L-band technology.

title: LatConnect 60 Looks to Raise Up to $20M in Growth Round to Fund SWIR Constellation
summary: LatConnect 60 is seeking to raise between $5 million and $20 million in a growth round to fund its short-wave infrared (SWIR) satellite constellation. The company aims to accelerate its expansion through this funding initiative.

title: SSC and SpaceWERX to Launch Orbital Logistics Challenge
summary: The U.S. Space Force’s Space Systems Command (SSC) and SpaceWERX are launching an In-Domain Orbital Logistics Challenge to advance commercial technologies for satellite sustainment. The initiative aims to identify and foster innovations in orbital logistics.
